Ad Text:
About DBG Health & Axe Health Services:   
At DBG Health, we are Defined by Good. Guided by a simple but powerful purpose: to create a world where feeling better is a right, not a privilege. Every day, millions of people rely on us for access to affordable, innovative health, wellness, and beauty solutions that make a genuine impact. Headquartered in Melbourne, we are a global-scale, privately owned organisation, powered by over 1,500 passionate professionals, united by a mission to help people feel better, live well, and thrive.   
 
Axe Health Services, a subsidiary of DBG Health, delivers a full spectrum of strategic solutions tailored for pharmacies and the pharmaceutical industry. Our unified service platforms provide a streamlined and cohesive multi-channel approach to health service implementation through our business' units, which include PharmaPrograms, Farmaforce, Sinapse Health, Praxhub, Wellness, and Precision Health. 
 
About The Role   
 
The Business Analyst, Sinapse Health supports the delivery of analytical, advisory, and improvement initiatives that enable evidence‐based decision making across the organisation. This role contributes to diagnostic assessments, financial and operational analysis, modelling, and the preparation of ‑high-quality reports and insights.
 
Working closely with consultants, project leads, and ‑cross functional stakeholders, the Business Analyst helps identify improvement opportunities, streamline processes, and strengthen ‑data driven‑ planning
 
Role & Responsibilities 

Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis, build financial models and dashboards, and translate data into actionable recommendations.
Contribute to planning, execution, and documentation across multiple projects, ensuring timely and high-quality outputs.
Prepare presentations, reports, and visualisations that clearly communicate complex insights and recommendations.
Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to gather inputs, validate findings, and support workshops and discussions.
Manage and develop analytical talent through coaching, performance management, and knowledge sharing.
Adhere to policies and safety standards while actively building industry, technical, and organisational knowledge.
Skills & Experience  

Proven ability in data analysis, modelling, and problem-solving to generate actionable insights.
Ability to interpret complex financial, operational, and business data to support decision-making.
Strong written and verbal capability, with experience in presenting insights clearly to stakeholders.
Background in analytics, consulting, finance, or business improvement; healthcare or pharmaceutical experience preferred.
 Experience working cross-functionally, managing multiple priorities, and supporting outcomes in fast-paced environments.
Tertiary qualification in a relevant field, with willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required
Benefits    

Exclusive employee discounts via the DBG Health Employee Shop, including MCoBeauty, Esmi Skin Minerals, Nude by Nature, e.l.f. Cosmetics, Chemist's Own and more.  
Acacia EAP Wellbeing Support, providing confidential 24/7 counselling, mental health support, financial coaching, and manager assistance.  
Join a values-driven, supportive, and friendly culture within a dynamic, fast-growing business that actively promotes professional development and internal mobility.  
Enjoy the ability to work autonomously in an empowering environment that supports ownership, accountability and personal success.  
Access to the Bastas Academy for Health Leadership (via Melbourne Business School), supporting emerging and senior leaders with industry-leading programs, coaching, and development.  
DBG Health Leadership Academy, offering structured leadership development, mentoring, and clear career progression pathways.  
DBG Health is committed to fostering a workplace culture that is safe and accessible for all. We are an equal- opportunity employer and encourage applications regardless of cultural background, gender identity, sexual orientation, disability or neurodiversity. We strive to build a collaborative, inclusive workplace that is representative of the communities we work and live in.

At Bob Jane T-Marts we have a total commitment to effectively serve the motorists of Australia with a level of service beyond their expectations, using the highest quality products at the most competitive price.

ABOUT THE ROLE:

Bob Jane T-Marts is entering its next phase of operational and commercial evolution. As our National Commercial Analyst – Business Operations, you will sit at the centre of decision-making across our national network.

This is not a back-office reporting role.

You will work directly with the Chief Operating Officer, State Teams, Regional Managers and the National Support Centre to turn data into action, financial insight into operational change, and AI capability into real business outcomes.

You will own the analysis of financial and operational performance across company-owned and franchise stores, identify inefficiencies, build meaningful cost and productivity metrics, and help embed AI tools and automation into how we run the business.

If you can read a P&L, question it, explain it simply, and then help a team fix it — this role is built for you.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:

Commercial & Financial Analysis

Analyse P&Ls, balance sheets and cash flow across company-owned and franchise businesses

Identify trends, risks, inefficiencies and opportunities at store, region, state and national level

Build and maintain commercial performance dashboards and reporting frameworks

Develop and track cost metrics, margin drivers, wage efficiency and productivity measures

Support pricing, promotional and commercial decisions with data-driven insight

Provide clear, practical recommendations that State and Regional teams can action

AI & Automation Integration

Identify opportunities to deploy AI tools and automation across:

Commercial reporting

Operational workflows

Forecasting and scenario modelling

Labour and productivity analysis

Work with internal stakeholders to integrate AI into day-to-day decision-making, not just theory

Improve speed, accuracy and consistency of reporting across the network

Act as a bridge between commercial insight, operations and emerging technology

Operational & Cross-Functional Support

Partner with teams across:

Finance

Operations

Property & network development

Marketing

WH&S and compliance

Analyse operational performance and link it back to financial outcomes

Support business cases for new initiatives, investments and network changes

Review store-level execution against strategy and identify improvement opportunities

Stakeholder Engagement & Governance

Conduct weekly and monthly performance reviews with the COO

Present insights and recommendations to senior leadership and state teams

Support Regional and State Managers with commercial insight to drive accountability

Translate complex data into clear, simple language for non-financial stakeholders

Travel nationally to support states, regions and major initiatives as required

 

WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR:

Experience & Capability

Proven experience in a commercial analyst or business operations analyst role

Strong capability in analysing P&Ls, balance sheets and multi-site retail or franchise networks

Experience working with large operational datasets and turning them into insight

Exposure to AI, automation or advanced analytics in a practical business setting

Retail, franchise, multi-site or operationally complex business experience highly regarded

Technical Skills

Advanced Excel / data modelling skills

Strong reporting and dashboarding capability (Power BI, Tableau or similar preferred)

Commercial acumen — understands how decisions impact profitability and cash

Ability to build metrics that actually drive behaviour, not just reporting

Personal Attributes

Commercially curious — asks why, not just what

Confident communicator who can challenge constructively

Comfortable working with senior leaders and frontline teams alike

Highly organised, self-driven and outcomes-focused

Adaptable and comfortable working across diverse parts of the business




WHY JOIN BOB JANE T-MARTS:

Work directly with the COO and senior leadership

Influence decisions across a national retail and franchise network

Play a key role in shaping how AI and data are used in a large Australian business

Broad exposure across finance, operations, property, marketing and safety

A role with real authority, visibility and impact — not just reporting for reporting’s sake

If you’re self-motivated, thrive in a fast-paced environment, and are ready to lead one of the best tyre retail teams in Australia, then this is your moment. Apply today and drive your career forward with Bob Jane T-Marts!

Ad Text:
Senior Business Analyst – Process Simplification

9 Month FTC

Hybrid opportunity | Central CBD Location 

Recognised by WORK180 as an employer of choice

V/Line’s purpose is to connect Victorians through safe, reliable, and accessible transport. We believe in giving all Victorians opportunities through travel and transport – connecting them to jobs, education, family and friends, services, tourism and trade. 

V/Line is experiencing significant growth and we’re delivering more services, more trains, cheaper fares, and huge projects. With more than 3700 weekly train and coach services, an expansive freight network and unprecedented rail investment, millions of Victorians depend on us to deliver. You could be one of our 3100 committed team members who are up to the challenge. 

About the role 

The Senior Business Analyst leads the Procurement Process Simplification stream within the Procurement Transformation Program. The program aims to improve sourcing and contract management by simplifying processes, improving system usability and ensuring strong governance and data foundations.

In this role, you will drive end‑to‑end process redesign, working closely with a Human‑Centred Design specialist vendor to understand user pain points and translate insights into practical, high‑value improvements. You will produce high‑quality requirements that inform systems optimisation initiatives and ensure alignment with policy, controls, data standards and the target operating model.

About You 

You are a senior, delivery‑focused business analyst who enjoys tackling complex problems and designing practical solutions. You bring strong user empathy, structure and analytical rigour, and are comfortable working across policy, process, systems and data. You communicate clearly, facilitate confidently, and are motivated by driving outcomes that genuinely improve user experience and compliance.

Our ideal candidate will have:  

Senior‑level business analysis experience, including procurement or adjacent process re‑engineering and systems‑enabled transformation.

Demonstrated expertise in enterprise‑grade requirements management and process design.

Hands‑on experience collaborating with UX/HCD teams to translate insights into implementable solutions.

Strong understanding of procurement pathways, approvals and contract lifecycle governance.

If your skills or experience don’t exactly match our listed requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We value diverse perspectives and believe in the potential for growth and development. 

Why V/Line 

In addition to free myki travel, we offer: 

Community impact – Make a genuine difference. Work in a role that connects Victorians, every day. 

Huge potential – We’re transforming, and you can too. Be a change maker, recognised for your contribution and creativity. 

Dynamic environment – V/Line operates in a constantly changing industry. Join a fast-paced and exciting organisation. 

Collaborative team – You’ll be part of a broader supportive team. Surround yourself with talented, passionate peers. 

Flexible working – We support flexible working. Work in a way that supports you to thrive in your role. 

We are committed to ensuring a comfortable and accessible recruitment experience. If you need any adjustments or have specific accessibility requirements at any stage of the process, please contact our Talent team at TalentSupport@vline.com.au or 1800 800 007 for confidential support. 

As an equal opportunity employer, V/Line is committed to creating a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace and network that reflects the communities we serve. We accept and value the contributions of all our employees regardless of gender, ethnicity, sexual orientation, religion, age or disability. We are also proud to be endorsed by Work 180 as a great place for women to work, second year in a row.  

We proudly acknowledge First Peoples of Victoria. We acknowledge their ongoing strength in practicing the world’s oldest living culture. We acknowledge the Traditional Owners’ lands, waters, and skies on which we live and pay respects to their Elders past and present.  

At V/Line, safety is our priority. Successful applicants will undergo relevant pre-employment and medical checks to meet our safe working standards. 

Applications close: Friday 17th April, 2026

Job Title: Project Lead, AI Operations | 3 month FTC
Ad Text:
Terrific opportunity for a Strategic Project Lead, AI Operations to step into a brand new and fast moving function supporting the rollout of AI across a cutting edge global business. The role focuses on establishing operational cadence, aligning multiple departments, and embedding decentralised AI workflows across the business. This position acts as the “connective tissue” between departments to ensure consistency in execution, communication, and progress against broader AI strategy.
This three month FTC needs for someone who can quickly understand the landscape, build momentum, and help establish an operating rhythm from the ground up.

About the role
You will act as the key connector between departments, helping align strategy, systems, workflows, and communication across several teams working at different stages of AI adoption.
This is not a traditional project role. You will be expected to think across systems and processes, identify blockers, challenge where needed, and push cross functional work forward. 

Key Responsibilities

Define and launch the AI operations process; establish cadence, governance, and communication rhythm.
Run regular alignment sessions with 3 to 4 departments progressing at different stages of AI adoption.
Identify key stakeholders and establish clear communication and decision pathways.
Identify technical or structural blockers and collaborate with subject matter experts to resolve.
Facilitate access, training, and cross department connectors between systems.
Design, test, and improve interdepartmental connectors (APIs, automations, shared workflows).
Push project timelines forward and ensure teams deliver multi department updates on schedule.
Promote a unified communication style across teams through informal engagement sessions (e.g. lunch and learns).
Share success stories, learnings, and improvement opportunities across technical and non technical groups.
Support departmental embedded engineers and ensure they can execute their part of the AI strategy.
Identify skill gaps and coordinate with other teams to bridge them.
Ideal Background

Experienced in AI driven process design, automation, or operational project leadership.
Background in business analysis or systems and process mapping, able to think across technical and business contexts.
Experience working within fast paced tech environments.
Hands on understanding of connectors, workflows, and APIs (out of the box solutions vs. custom built).
Demonstrated experience in building or enhancing AI workflows, ideally with examples of tools or solutions personally developed or configured.
Personal Attributes

Adaptable, comfortable with ambiguity and fast changing priorities.
Confident challenging stakeholders constructively and keeping momentum despite obstacles.
Collaborative communicator who can engage both technical and non technical audiences.
Background in business analysis, operational coordination, or project delivery in a fast paced global environment.
Exposure to AI tools, workflow automation, or connected systems.
Experience working with cross functional teams and multiple stakeholders.
Ability to assess technical blockers and work through them with the right people.
Experience in environments where you have had to push hard to keep projects moving.
In this role, success will mean quickly understanding the current landscape, building trust across teams, creating structure where needed, and helping drive AI related initiatives forward with clarity and momentum.
If you are a hands on Project Leader / Business Analyst who enjoys bringing structure to new initiatives and working across teams to make things happen, please apply via the link or email jqiugley@sbrecruitment.com

Ad Text:
About Us

Globally, energy markets are in transition to a net zero future and the entities within these markets are transforming their underlying business models to ensure that they stay relevant, sustainable and profitable. As one of Australia's largest energy providers, we are proud of our purpose to lead and accelerate the clean energy transformation. Our ability to deliver on this purpose will be dependent on our people.

As the energy sector rapidly evolves with electrification, renewables, and sustainability at the forefront, we are delivering innovative centralised energy solutions to meet the needs of modern communities.

Role Purpose

Joining our high-trust, highly collaborative and ambitious Trading and Transition business unit, you will play a key role in driving EnergyAustralia's transition to a cleaner energy future.

Forming part of our Portfolio Development Team, the role of the Portfolio Development Advisor enables all your commercial experience, market knowledge, and passion for the clean energy transition to come together to perform the commercial activities required to support large-scale development projects and deliver our future energy portfolio. Essentially, you will perform the commercial activities required to deliver the future energy portfolio for EnergyAustralia. This could include projects such as Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS), Pumped Hydro Energy Storage (PHES), and Gas-Fired Turbine Generation.

Key Duties & Accountabilities

Support the origination of new projects by focusing on financial opportunities and structures
Work closely with Development Director's to originate and execute portfolio development projects
Provide commercial support to senior Portfolio Development team members
Develop and maintain integrated financial models to support business decisions
Present to and influence internal and external stakeholders, whilst developing and maintaining strong networks

About You

With your relevant experience in infrastructure, energy, or resources and / or roles in banking or consulting supporting clients in these industries, you will know how critical it is to draw upon your foundational corporate finance and commercial skillset to execute effective deal structuring and development support.

In addition to these rich commercial attributes, we are seeking the following experience, skills, and qualifications:

Relevant tertiary qualifications in business, finance, economics, engineering, law or similar
Advanced Excel and VBA skills with strong proficiency in financial modelling including developing macros and automating model workflows
Strong understanding of project finance structuring including SPV structures, limited recourse debt, revenue contracting mechanisms and equity return metrics
Understanding of the Australian energy sector including NEM/WEM market frameworks, CIS/LTESA mechanisms and the renewable energy project development lifecycle
Strong communication and presentation skills, with the ability to convey financial analysis to both technical and non-technical audiences
Excellent interpersonal and teamwork skills with experience managing internal & external stakeholders and navigating complex processes

Interested?

If you're ready to ‘light the way' towards your next career move, click the 'Apply' button before 11.55pm Tuesday 21st April.

Why Us?
EnergyAustralia is a place where all employees are genuinely valued, supported, and free to be themselves — because we believe that's when we're at our best.

We embrace diversity of background, experience, and perspective, and we encourage applications from Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with disability, and those from the LGBTQ+ community.

Here's what you can look forward to:

Employee discounts on electricity and gas, plus savings on major brands and products
20 weeks' paid parental leave - completely gender-equal
Energise Program - flexible working that's team-centric, enabling teams to agree and succeed together
Performance recognition and incentive programmes - tailored to different roles and teams to reward great work throughout the year
Supportive leaders and a down-to-earth culture where you can be your authentic self

About the role

As Principal Solutions Architect, you will set the architectural direction and provide strategic technical leadership for the Department’s operational tooling ecosystem, with a strong focus on ServiceNow and its integrations.

You will design and guide solutions that convert high volume‑, multisource telemetry into meaningful, actionable intelligence. This enables ‑Multivendor Environment (MVE) support teams to rapidly triage and resolve incidents, while maintaining an immutable, auditable source of truth to assure service performance and provider accountability‑.

The role plays a critical part in uplifting SIAM maturity, using data driven‑ insights to improve service outcomes across complex, highly regulated government environments.

Skills & Experience

Deep expertise in ServiceNow ITSM, including Event Management, Service Mapping and Advanced Performance Analytics.

Proven delivery of advanced analytics and reporting using ServiceNow and Power BI, producing executive, operational and practice level‑ insights.

Strong experience integrating ServiceNow with ScienceLogic (SL1), Splunk, and other operations platforms, with a focus on measuring and improving tool effectiveness.

Familiarity with modern monitoring and observability tools such as Prometheus, Splunk, ScienceLogic, Aternity, and cloud native‑ monitoring solutions.

Demonstrated success operating within Australian Government frameworks, managing complex systems with strict security, risk and compliance controls.

Experience supporting Multivendor‑ Environments (MVE) where disparate vendor tools must align to deliver end-to-end‑ services.

Ability to uplift SIAM practices through evidence based insights, metrics‑ and continuous improvement initiatives.

Must hold and maintain an NV1 Australian Government Security Clearance.

 Close off date

This role is being recruited with urgency. Applications close Monday night, 6 April 2026.

Senior Social Value Specialist - Permanent Full Time - Perth CBD



Be at the forefront of shaping the social legacy of Western Australia's largest energy infrastructure program.

As Senior Social Value Specialist, you will play a pivotal role in how Western Power plans, approves and delivers major transmission projects - ensuring social performance, community benefit and long‑term value are embedded from the outset. The role provides strategic social impact and social legacy expertise, supporting informed decision‑making, helping to de‑risk delivery, and strengthening Western Power's social licence across the SWIS.

Your Role in Action

Lead the development of social value and social legacy initiatives that guide major transmission projects across the SWIS.
Integrate social value considerations into early planning, design and delivery, ensuring community needs and expectations inform project decisions.
Oversee priority social impact insights and assessment activities ensuring work is robust, consistent and aligned with Western Power's social performance requirements.
Shape social procurement requirements by contributing to tender development, supplier management and contract performance measures related to social value.
Monitor and report on social value outcomes to support internal governance, external reporting and transparency with stakeholders.
Identify and support implementation of opportunities for local participation, capability building and community initiatives, working collaboratively with internal teams.
Partner closely with engagement teams, ensuring engagement insights and cultural considerations are reflected in project planning and delivery.
Provide strategic advice on emerging social risks and opportunities, including potential impacts on Western Power's reputation and social licence.
Collaborate across engineering environment, land procurement and project delivery teams to embed social value principles consistently throughout the program.
Manage and coordinate external specialists and delivery partners, ensuring their work supports program objectives and maintains required quality standards.

What Makes You a Great Fit

Tertiary qualification in social sciences, social planning, or related discipline
Extensive knowledge and expertise, with experience in delivering social impact assessment and social value programs within major infrastructure or similar large scale project environments.
Demonstrated capability in developing and implementing social value or community benefit strategies with measurable outcomes.
Well-developed stakeholder engagement and negotiation skills with the ability to work across diverse internal and external groups.
Strong analytical, reporting and problem-solving skills, including experience evaluating social performance data.
Experience influencing procurement processes and shaping supplier commitments related to social value.

Location: South Side Brisbane Tech Hub

Role Type: Permanent / Full-Time

The Opportunity

As a Senior Project Manager, you'll lead the end-to-end delivery of complex technology initiatives, ensuring outcomes are delivered safely, compliantly, and at pace. Beyond traditional delivery, you will play a pivotal role in shaping Consumer Finance projects, ensuring new capabilities-particularly those powered by AI and automation-are introduced responsibly to deliver genuine customer and business value.

You will be a cultural anchor for our South Side Brisbane Tech Hub, leading a vibrant environment of junior PMs and engineers by example, bringing the energy and drive necessary to thrive in a high-growth, modern setting.

What You'll Do

Dynamic Delivery: Own scope, schedule, budget, risk, and quality for complex initiatives, translating high-level business strategy into actionable delivery plans and measurable outcomes.

Navigate Disruption: Lead cross-functional teams (engineers, architects, data specialists, and vendors) through continuous shifts in technology stacks and "Ways of Working" (WoW).

Adaptive Risk Management: Move beyond "checkbox" governance. You will use lateral thinking to manage risks where traditional guardrails may not yet exist, ensuring safe delivery in an evolving AI-driven landscape.

AI & Automation Champion: Proactively identify where AI can optimize delivery efficiency or customer value, while managing the change impacts on team skills and customer experience.

Lead from the Front: Act as a mentor and coach within the South Side Brisbane hub, uplifting delivery maturity and sharing modern delivery practices to help the broader team grow.

What We're Looking For

We are looking for a leader who understands that in an AI-disrupted world, adaptability is the new gold standard.

Proven Delivery Excellence: Extensive experience managing complex tech projects within similar large-scale, regulated environments.

Lateral Thinking over Legacy Process: While you respect methodology, you don't rely on it as a crutch. You have the confidence to pivot and solve problems creatively when the standard process falls short.

Resilience Amidst Change: A track record of helping teams remain productive and focused despite constant shifts in tools, tech stacks, and methodologies.

High-Octane Leadership: You bring infectious energy and drive. You are comfortable being a visible leader in a hub environment, coaching junior talent and leading from the front to set the pace.

Ad Text:
About Liontown

Liontown Limited (ASX: LTR) is one of Australia's most exciting emerging lithium producers. Our Kathleen Valley Lithium Project in Western Australia is in active production ramp-up, supplying high-quality spodumene concentrate to global battery supply chains. As we grow from developer to producer, we're building the team and capability to match our ambition.

The Opportunity

We're looking for an experienced IR professional to own and lead Liontown's investor relations function. You'll be the primary interface between our Executive Leadership Team and the investment community — shaping how domestic and international capital markets understand and value our story.

Reporting directly to the CFO, you'll work closely with the CEO/MD, Company Secretary, and Board, and play a central role in all capital markets activity as Liontown continues to grow.

What You'll Do

Develop and execute Liontown's IR strategy, aligned with corporate and capital markets objectives.
Build and maintain relationships with institutional investors, retail shareholders, and equity analysts domestically and internationally.
Lead investor engagement programs including roadshows, conferences, site visits, and results briefings.
Support continuous disclosure obligations — drafting and coordinating ASX announcements, quarterly reports, results materials, and investor presentations.
Monitor and analyse the investment community's perceptions of Liontown, including shifts in institutional interest, short positions, and shareholder register movements; provide regular reporting to ELT and Board.
Track equity research coverage and analyst models, maintaining constructive relationships with covering analysts to ensure accuracy and consistency with ASX announcements.
Monitor competitor activity, sector news, and macroeconomic developments relevant to the lithium and battery materials sector — synthesising intelligence and briefing leadership on emerging themes.
Own the IR digital presence including the Liontown website, LinkedIn, and shareholder communications.
Support capital markets transactions including equity raises, analyst days, and strategic investor processes — coordinating roadshow programs with corporate advisors, brokers, and custodian banks.
Prepare executive briefing packs, Q&A documents, and talking points ahead of investor-facing engagements.

What We're Looking For

Essential:

7+ years' experience in investor relations, equity capital markets, corporate advisory, or financial communications.
Demonstrated IR experience in an ASX-listed company, ideally resources, mining, or materials.
Strong working knowledge of ASX Listing Rules, continuous disclosure obligations, and ASIC guidance.
Credible communicator with institutional investors, analysts, and corporate advisors.
Outstanding written skills — able to translate complex financial and technical information into compelling narratives.

Highly Regarded:

Familiarity with the lithium, critical minerals, or battery materials sector.
Experience supporting capital markets transactions or major operational transitions.
Exposure to international IR programs and ESG reporting frameworks (TCFD, GRI).
CFA, MBA, or postgraduate qualification advantageous.

Why Liontown

Be part of a genuine growth story at a critical inflection point — production ramp-up, global interest, and strategic momentum.
Direct access to and influence with the CEO/MD, CFO, and Board.
Perth-based with domestic and international travel.
A values-led culture grounded in Safety, Integrity, Ambition, and Respect.

How to Apply

If you're ready to shape the investor story of one of Australia's most talked-about lithium producers, we'd love to hear from you.

Apply with your CV and a brief cover letter outlining your experience and what draws you to this role.

Job Title: RFQ EL1 IM Policy, Governance & Compliance Lead
Ad Text:
Your new company

You'll be joining a specialist consulting environment working with a large, complex Australian Government agency in a data‑intensive context. The organisation is undertaking a significant uplift in its information and data management maturity, with a strong focus on legislative compliance, digital continuity and practical, staff‑friendly governance. This work sits at the intersection of policy, technology and operational reality, and is sponsored at senior executive level.

Your new role

As a senior Information Management / Records Management specialist, you will lead the development of contemporary, APS‑aligned IM policy and compliance artefacts and provide expert advice on records management capability across Microsoft 365.



Key responsibilities include:

Reviewing existing IM policy artefacts and conducting a structured gap analysis, including IM Policy, Normal Administrative Practice Policy, Digitise to Dispose Policy, IM Governance Framework and IM Strategy and Delivery Plan
Designing and authoring a new Information and Data Archival Policy and associated governance artefacts from first principles, aligned to the Archives Act 1983 and Privacy Act 1988
Producing clear, practical, plain‑English staff guidance for managing information in Outlook, SharePoint Online, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive, network drives and Microsoft Purview
Ensuring all artefacts align with the Archives Act 1983, Privacy Act 1988, PSPF, NAA Digital Continuity 2025 and relevant APS standards
Facilitating policy review and endorsement sessions with governance, legal, enterprise architecture and cyber security stakeholders
Assessing SharePoint Online and Microsoft Purview against NAA records management requirements to identify gaps, risks and remediation options
Engaging with ICT Solution Front Door processes and IM, architecture, security and operations teams to validate technology feasibility
Delivering a structured Records Management Compliance Assessment Report with risk‑rated findings and recommendations
Contributing IM policy and compliance expertise to pilot lessons‑learned and compliance monitoring frameworks

What you'll need to succeed


To be successful in this role, you will bring:

7+ years' experience in APS information management, records management or IM compliance roles, ideally in health, statistics or other data‑intensive environments
Deep working knowledge of the Archives Act 1983, Privacy Act 1988, PSPF and NAA Digital Continuity 2025
Demonstrated experience authoring APS‑standard policies, governance frameworks and practical staff guidance that is genuinely adopted
Proven ability to conduct formal IM or records management compliance assessments with risk‑rated findings
Strong written communication skills, with experience producing SES‑facing material independently
Hands‑on understanding of SharePoint Online and Microsoft Purview from a governance and records compliance perspective
Familiarity with Microsoft Purview retention and disposition capabilities
Experience engaging with APS IT governance and Solution Front Door‑style architecture review processes
Awareness of emerging AI and Microsoft Copilot governance considerations for information management
Relevant qualifications in records management, information management, library science, law or a related discipline (desirable)

What you'll get in return

You'll have the opportunity to work on high-impact, nationally significant IM initiatives, with strong executive sponsorship and access to senior stakeholders. This role offers a chance to shape enduring policy and governance foundations, influence technology decisions, and apply best-practice information management in a modern Microsoft 365 environment.

About Us

Workwear Group (WWG) is part of Wesfarmers Industrial & Safety a division of Wesfarmers, a top 20 ASX listed company with over 100,000 employees. Housed under the banner of Workwear Group, our three iconic brands - KingGee, Hard Yakka and NNT - have been in the business of delivering workwear since the late 1920's. 

We are the trusted partner in workwear solutions, manufacturing over seven million units each year. What sets us apart is our continuous investment in innovation, market knowledge, pioneering ideas and sustainability practices.

We work as one team. We put our customers first and are passionate about what we do. Joining WWG is an opportunity to thrive in an inspiring, safe, and inclusive community.

About the Role

As a Senior Integration Engineer, you will design, deliver, and maintain robust integration solutions that connect internal and external applications, with a strong focus on B2C and B2B e-commerce platforms. Leveraging Workato as the primary integration platform, alongside Microsoft Azure and other modern technologies, you’ll ensure seamless data flow, security, and scalability across critical business systems.

You’ll work closely with Product Owners, engineering teams, and business stakeholders to translate requirements into secure, high-performing solutions. This role demands expertise in API development, data transformation, and cloud integration, combined with strong problem-solving skills to optimize and troubleshoot complex integrations. Your work will underpin the digital backbone of our operations, enabling innovation and exceptional customer experiences.

What you’ll be doing

Design, develop, and maintain integration solutions using Workato and Azure technologies to connect internal and external systems.
Collaborate with business stakeholders and technical teams to gather requirements and translate them into secure, scalable integration designs.
Build and manage APIs, ensuring compliance with authentication protocols and data security standards.
Monitor and optimize existing integrations for performance, reliability, and scalability.
Troubleshoot and resolve integration issues, including root cause analysis and implementing long-term fixes.
Ensure all incidents and service requests are resolved within agreed SLAs, maintaining clear communication with stakeholders.
Implement ETL processes and data transformation workflows to support accurate and efficient data exchange.
Participate in architecture reviews and present integration designs for approval and alignment with enterprise standards.
Support CI/CD pipelines and version control practices to enable continuous improvement and rapid deployment of integration solutions.
Provide after-hours support when required and maintain disaster recovery plans to ensure business continuity.

Who we’re looking for

At our company, we value not only our great brands but also the people and teams that drive our success. In addition to technical skills, we seek team members who possess the following personable qualities:

Strong work ethic and are committed to showing up for a team
Openness to receive coaching and embrace self reflection and self growth
Accountability for your actions, and emotions, and actively venture beyond your comfort zone
Embrace innovation, take calculated risks, and inspire others to think outside the box
Ability to build meaningful relationships, we prioritise our customers. Balancing commercial goals with customer satisfaction is key
Ethical standards and demonstrating honesty and integrity in all interactions is fundamental to our team culture.

What we’re looking for

Proven experience designing and delivering integrations using Workato (working knowledge required) and Azure Logic Apps.
Strong understanding of API development and management, including RESTful and SOAP services, authentication protocols (OAuth, JWT), and secure data exchange.
Proficiency in programming and scripting languages such as Java, Python, Ruby, or JavaScript for automation and data transformation.
Hands-on experience with ETL processes, data mapping, and working with formats like XML, JSON, and SQL.
Solid knowledge of cloud integration principles, particularly Azure services, and familiarity with security controls and compliance frameworks.
Ability to troubleshoot, optimize, and maintain integration solutions, ensuring scalability, reliability, and performance.

What we offer

The chance to join a great team of like-minded, hard-working people who are passionate about achieving our vision and the growth objectives of the company.
Flexible working environment, allowing for work from home day/s each week.
A competitive salary package and suite of benefits and perks, including a 50% discount on our brands Hard Yakka, KingGee and NNT and 5% Wesfarmers discount for Kmart, Bunnings, Officeworks and more.
A tailored career and development plan, plus reward and recognition programs. Whatever your goals we’re here to support you on that journey.
Our Wellbeing program, Thrive, with activities designed to support the Financial Wellness of our team, Celebrate Diversity, ensure we Get Physical and maintain Healthy Minds, including challenges, yoga, meditation and access to our Employee Assistance Program.
We Learn, a dedicated hub for all things learning and development.

We actively work on creating a safe and inclusive environment free of discrimination. At Workwear Group we aspire to build a culture where diversity is celebrated, where Team Members feel a sense of “I Belong” and are empowered to bring their whole selves to work.

You can follow us on LinkedIn or see our company careers page for more opportunities https://www.workweargroup.com.au/careers

At Uniting, our purpose is simple but powerful: to inspire people, enliven communities and confront injustice. We’re an organisation that challenges the status quo and works where we’re needed most, and our technology and systems play a critical role in enabling that impact.

This role sits within the Care and Clinical Systems & Reporting team and is accountable for the end-to-end oversight, optimisation and governance of key client management systems used across Communities and Property services.

You will lead a team of four and operate with a high level of autonomy, driving solution design (within vendor constraints), prioritisation, release management and delivery coordination through established governance frameworks. 

The role requires a strong balance of technical capability, business analysis, leadership and stakeholder engagement, with a focus on delivering stable, secure and continuously improving systems that enable better outcomes for clients and frontline teams.  

Key responsibilities 

Leadership & team capability

Apply fit-for-purpose project and release management practices across all system changes  
Oversee UAT and SIT processes, ensuring robust testing, defect management and quality assurance  
Drive continuous improvement initiatives, identifying opportunities to streamline processes and enhance system capability  

Stakeholder & vendor engagement 

Build strong, trusted relationships with stakeholders across operational teams, Digital Technology and external vendors  
Lead user forums and engagement channels to capture insights, issues and innovation opportunities  
Influence decision-making and manage competing priorities across a complex organisational landscape  

Integration & enterprise alignment 

Ensure effective integration and connectivity across systems and platforms  
Actively manage system interdependencies, risks and issues  
Partner across Uniting to deliver seamless, end-to-end service outcomes  

About you 

You are a confident and capable systems leader who thrives in complexity and is motivated by purpose-driven work. 

You will bring: 

5+ years’ experience in enterprise systems, business technology or a related discipline  
Demonstrated experience working with client management systems and complex system environments

Strong experience in data governance, reporting and system integrity  
Experience delivering end-to-end system enhancements, upgrades and business solutions  
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to influence across all levels

Highly regarded:

Relevant tertiary qualifications in IT, systems, education or a related field  

Why join Uniting? 

Be part of an organisation that is deeply committed to social impact  
Lead meaningful work that directly supports better outcomes for communities  
Work in a values-driven culture: Compassionate, Respectful, Imaginative and Bold  
Enjoy hybrid working and flexibility  
Free onsite parking at our North Parramatta campus

If you’re looking for a role where you can combine leadership, systems thinking and purpose, this is an opportunity to make a genuine difference.

At Uniting, we work across aged care, disability, mental health, community services and social justice. We welcome people from all backgrounds and are committed to creating a safe, inclusive workplace for everyone. 

Ready to apply?

Meaningful work that matters — build a rewarding career with one of Australia’s leading human services organisations  
More money in your pocket — access up to $18,550 in tax-free salary packaging as part of our NFP benefits  
Wellbeing that goes beyond work — access Fitness Passport and prioritise your health your way

Empowered by diversity

Candidates must be an Australian Citizen and already hold a current AGSVA TSPV security clearance to commence work in this role. Please clearly indicate the current level held within your responses.

Job Description

As a Senior Strategy Manager at SEEK, you'll play a pivotal role in defining and delivering our long-term vision and growth ambitions. Focused on topics within SEEK's APAC product strategy as part of the Strategy Operations Centre of Gravity, you'll tackle some of our most critical and complex challenges, spanning multiple product and AI domains.

You'll lead deep strategic thinking, apply analytical rigour, and turn insights into action. This role suits someone who thrives in ambiguity, is energised by solving complex business problems, and is excited by the opportunity to shape SEEK's future at scale.

What You'll Be Doing

Sitting at the intersection of Product, Commercial, and Global Supply Chain, this role drives tight alignment between the product roadmap and supply chain strategies to ensure the right decisions are made at the right time—backed by insight, data, and cross‑functional partnership.

You will shape and execute product lifecycle strategies from new product launch through to end-of-life.  This includes enabling successful product launches, optimizing inventory for new and obsolescent products, and proactively managing risk while unlocking opportunities for profitable growth.

Working closely with cross‑functional stakeholders, you will lead demand and supply planning discussions for new and obsolescent products—challenging assumptions, improving forecast accuracy, and translating complexity into clear, actionable plans.

By leveraging advanced analytics, strong commercial acumen and deep expertise in Supply and Demand Planning you will deliver product lifecycle strategies that have a measurable impact in a dynamic and rapidly evolving MedTech landscape.

Let’s Talk About Responsibilities:

Strategic Lifecycle Planning

Lead the design and execution of end-to-end product lifecycle strategies that align with portfolio priorities, revenue growth, cost efficiencies, and competitiveness.

Ensure lifecycle decisions maximize customer value, safeguard availability, and support profitable growth.

Identify and mitigate supply chain risks to enable seamless new product introductions, scaling, and discontinuations.

Integrate commercial insights into demand forecasts with clear assumptions and robust review cycles.

Develop inventory strategies that balance availability with efficiency, including last-time buy planning for components tied to end-of-life products.

Monitor and analyze key performance indicators (KPIs) to assess NPI forecast accuracy post launch

Review NPI Business Case and Ramp Plans and provide inputs into initial demand forecasts based on historical data, and inputs from Product teams including assumptions.

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather relevant information, such as sales forecasts, inventory levels, and promotional activities, to enhance demand planning accuracy.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Build trusted partnerships with Commercial, Product, Project, and Supply Chain teams to deliver timely supply outcomes aligned with lifecycle strategies.

Drive alignment across functions on:

Supply chain and project risk management, with mitigation plans.

Demand assumptions, uncertainties, and changes.

Market dynamics and regional strategies.

Foster accountability with clear ownership through strong stakeholder engagement.

Communicate lifecycle plans and updates to business stakeholders, providing data-driven insights and recommendations.

Represent the supply chain function in product development reviews, project gate milestones, and commercial strategy sessions.

Commercial Acumen & Market Intelligence

Translate business strategy and customer needs into actionable planning initiatives.

Apply financial modelling, ROI analysis, and scenario planning to optimize lifecycle decisions.

Anticipate and adapt to market changes with data-driven insights.

Innovation & Process Optimization

Challenge the status quo with new ideas that drive transformation, cost optimization, and process excellence.

Partner with Planning Excellence to support Supply Chain initiatives.

Data-Driven Decision Making

Champion a data-centric approach to lifecycle planning by leveraging advanced analytics, predictive modelling, and real-time data from supply chain systems to inform decisions.

Confidently influence stakeholders by presenting evidence-based recommendations.

Success Metrics

On-time NPI delivery with full supply chain readiness.

Smooth execution of end-of-life and obsolescence plans without disruption.

Optimized inventory and costs

Strong stakeholder feedback on influence and collaboration.

Proactive lifecycle risk identification and mitigation.

Contributions to process improvements and digital supply chain initiatives.

Let’s Talk About You:

Education: Bachelor’s in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Engineering, or related field (MBA preferred).

Experience: 5+ years in supply chain planning with strong demand planning and product lifecycle management experience and at least 2 years in MedTech or regulated healthcare.

Expertise in forecasting methodologies and tools.

Commercial Acumen: Strong financial analysis, forecasting, and P&L alignment.

Analytical Skills: Skilled in scenario modelling, trend analysis, and visualization tools (Excel, Power BI, Tableau).

Technical Skills: Proficiency in ERP (SAP, Oracle), planning tools, and data analytics platforms.

Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, dynamic environment and drive continuous improvement initiatives.

Detail-oriented with a strategic mindset, capable of balancing short-term needs with long-term objectives.

Soft Skills: Exceptional communication, stakeholder management, and influencing skills across all levels of the business.

Preferred: APICS CPIM/CSCP or PMP certification, agile product development experience, and exposure to sustainable or digital supply chain initiatives.
